title: Dynamic multi-light array (torches, muzzle, projectile glow, explosions)
description: q1k3 lights the world with a per-fragment point-light array (up to ~32). The port has a single follow-light. Add: a lit-shader prelude light loop + uniform array, a pure light-accumulation module with q1k3 distance fade (768-1024) and RGB-nibble color, and wire lights from muzzle flashes, projectile glow (plasma/grenade), explosions, and torches. See q1k3 renderer.js r_push_light and entity_light.js.
close_reason: Dynamic point-light array implemented: pure light/pack-lights, shader prelude loop, player lamp + muzzle flash + projectile glow + explosion flashes. Torches still pending (world-objects issue).

title: Full weapon + pickup set (nailgun, grenade launcher, switching)
description: Add the nailgun and grenade-launcher weapons (projectile.clj already has nail/grenade), weapon switching (mouse wheel / keys), ammo tracking, and the remaining pickups: nailgun, grenade launcher, nails (+50), grenades (+10), key. q1k3 weapons.js + entity_pickup_*.js. HUD ammo readout should show real counts for ammo weapons.

title: World objects: barrels, doors, torches, lights, level trigger
description: Port the non-enemy map entities: entity_barrel (explodes, chain damage), entity_door (opens near player, key-gated on m1), entity_torch (wall-mounted flicker light), entity_light (flicker/temporary), entity_trigger_level (next-level volume). Spawned from decoded map entity ids 11-16. Torches/lights depend on the dynamic light array.

title: Distinct per-type enemy meshes
description: All enemy types currently render with unit.rmf (the grunt mesh). q1k3 has 8 model geometries + gib pieces (model_data[0..7]). Pack the other .obj models via the rmf pipeline (enforcer/ogre/zombie reuse the humanoid at different scales; hound has its own; barrel/torch/pickups too) and select the mesh per enemy :model. Assets in q1k3/assets/models.

title: Audio: sfx synthesis + music
description: q1k3 synthesizes all sfx (audio.js audio_create_sound) and a song (music.js) via WebAudio. jolt/glimmer has no audio bindings yet, so this needs a native audio backend (e.g. CoreAudio/OpenAL bindings) plus a port of the sound-generation params. Largest remaining track; lowest gameplay-blocking priority.

title: Second level (m2) + level transitions + THE END
description: The container already decodes both m1 and m2. Add level switching (game_next_level via entity_trigger_level), rebuild geometry/collision/entities on transition, map-1 starting-yaw rotation, and the THE END screen after m2. See q1k3 game.js game_run/game_next_level.
